full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding assumptions behind kernel learning in the RKL framework
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What assumptions does the Radius-based Kernel Learning method rely on? Are there implicit assumptions about the kernel family or the data?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped identify that the method assumes the linear combination of basis kernels is expressive enough for the task, and that the MEB radius faithfully represents data spread.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I confirmed these assumptions by reading Section 2 (Eq. 2) and Section 2.2 (Eqs. 7–8) of the paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Not applicable because only conceptual explanations were written.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking whether kernel matrices must be positive definite for the method to work
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Does the kernel matrix K(theta) need to be strictly positive definite for the RKL algorithm? What happens if it is only positive semi-definite?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that strict positive definiteness is required to ensure unique solutions in both the SVM and MEB dual problems, which in turn guarantees that the top-level objective g(theta) is continuously differentiable (Theorem 1, Section 4).
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the paragraph following Theorem 1 in Section 4, which explicitly states this requirement.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Not applicable because only conceptual explanations were written.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the role of the Minimum Enclosing Ball and when its radius may be misleading
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What role does the Minimum Enclosing Ball play in the RKL formulation? Can outliers in feature space cause problems for this approach?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained that the MEB radius R²(k) is used to normalise the margin in the objective (Eq. 9), and that outliers can inflate R²(k), distorting the optimisation objective and potentially leading to poor kernel selection.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I verified by reviewing Equations 7–8 in Section 2.2, which define the MEB primal and dual, and considered how a single far-away point would dominate the dual solution.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Not applicable because only conceptual explanations were written.
